title: L5: Dashboards & Charts
description: Build dashboard evidence for BTEC DIT Component 2 by choosing suitable charts, preparing chart source tables and presenting information clearly.

steps.title: Do Now: Data Preparation
steps.content:
Do now

Dashboard work depends on accurate analysis and well-prepared data. Check those skills first.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l5-dn',[{q:'Which function counts how many rows have Region as North?',options:['COUNTIF','SUMIF','MAX','AVERAGE'],answer:0,right:'Correct. COUNTIF counts matching entries.'},{q:'Which function adds Income only for Online sales?',options:['SUMIF','COUNTIF','MIN','IF'],answer:0,right:'Correct. SUMIF adds values where a condition is met.'},{q:'Why should a chart be based on a small summary table rather than a messy raw dataset?',options:['It makes the chart easier to understand and less likely to include the wrong data','It makes the file impossible to edit','It removes all formulas','It hides the worksheet'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Summary tables make chart data clear.'},{q:'Which feature makes values such as Shop sales stand out in a dataset?',options:['Conditional formatting','COUNT','Data labels','Pie chart'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Conditional formatting highlights values using rules.'},{q:'Which display is clearest for a share of total sales?',options:['Percentage','Random text','Unformatted decimal only','A hidden row'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Shares of a total are usually presented as percentages.'}]);

steps.title: What Makes a Good Dashboard?
steps.content:
What makes a good dashboard?

In BTEC Component 2, the dashboard is not just decoration. It should help the organisation understand important patterns from the data.

Good dashboardWeak dashboard
Shows key totals, counts and comparisons.Shows random data without a purpose.
Uses suitable charts for the question.Uses chart types that make values hard to compare.
Has clear titles, labels and units.Leaves the viewer guessing what the chart means.
Is readable and consistent.Uses cluttered colours and tiny text.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l5-purpose',[{q:'What is the main purpose of a dashboard?',options:['To present important information clearly for decision making','To store every raw row on one screen','To replace formulas','To make the file larger'],answer:0,right:'Correct. A dashboard should communicate useful information.'},{q:'A chart title says Sales. Why is that weak?',options:['It is too vague and does not say what sales are being shown','It has too many formulas','It is too colourful','It uses text'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Titles should be specific.'},{q:'Which dashboard element helps viewers understand exact values?',options:['Data labels or a clear value axis','Hidden gridlines only','Random colours','Blank rows'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Labels and axes make values easier to interpret.'}]);

steps.title: Choose the Right Chart
steps.content:
Choose the right chart

The chart type should match the question being answered. In BTEC dashboards, you often compare categories, show a proportion, or show change over time.

QuestionSuitable chartWhy
Which region has the most sales?Bar/column chartCompares categories clearly.
What percentage of sales are Online?Pie/doughnut chart or percentage cardShows share of a whole.
How did satisfaction change over four weeks?Line chartShows change over time.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l5-chart-choice',[{q:'You need to compare total income for Shop and Online. Which chart is most suitable?',options:['Bar or column chart','Line chart only','Scatter chart','No chart'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Bar/column charts compare categories well.'},{q:'You need to show how satisfaction ratings changed from Week 1 to Week 4. Which chart is most suitable?',options:['Line chart','Pie chart','Single text box','Sort button'],answer:0,right:'Correct. A line chart shows change over time.'},{q:'You need to show Online as a share of all sales. Which is suitable?',options:['Pie/doughnut chart or percentage card','A random image','A text-only formula cell','A sorted raw table only'],answer:0,right:'Correct. A share of the whole should be shown as a percentage or part-whole chart.'},{q:'Which chart feature should almost always be included?',options:['Clear title and labels','Hidden title','Unlabelled axis','Random colours'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Viewers need to know what they are looking at.'}]);

steps.title: Build Chart Source Tables
steps.content:
Build chart source tables

Charts are easiest to make from a small source table. Use formulas to count or total each category, then build the chart from that table.

Complete the source table in columns E and F. It will later become a chart comparing sales methods.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=17',function(){var data=[["Sale ID","Method","Income","","Method","Number of sales"],["S001","Shop",300,"","Shop",""] ,["S002","Online",820,"","Online",""] ,["S003","Shop",120,"","",""] ,["S004","Online",450,"","",""] ,["S005","Shop",240,"","",""]];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l5-source-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{width:90},{width:90},{width:90},{width:40},{width:100},{width:130}],minDimensions:[6,6],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l5-source-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l5-source-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l5-source-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l5-source-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l5-source-fb');var rShop=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(5,1,false)||'');var rOn=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(5,2,false)||'');var vShop=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(5,1,true));var vOn=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(5,2,true));var ok=rShop.charAt(0)==='='&&rOn.charAt(0)==='='&&rShop.toUpperCase().indexOf('COUNTIF')!==-1&&rOn.toUpperCase().indexOf('COUNTIF')!==-1&&Math.abs(vShop-3)<0.1&&Math.abs(vOn-2)<0.1;if(ok){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct. This two-row table can be used to create a clear sales-method chart.';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Use COUNTIF on the Method range B2:B6. Shop should count 3 and Online should count 2.';}};});});});})();

steps.title: Totals for Dashboard Charts
steps.content:
Totals for dashboard charts

Some charts need totals, not counts. For example, comparing income from Shop and Online sales needs SUMIF, because it adds income values for each method.

Complete the income source table in columns E and F.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=17',function(){var data=[["Sale ID","Method","Income","","Method","Income total"],["S001","Shop",300,"","Shop",""] ,["S002","Online",820,"","Online",""] ,["S003","Shop",120,"","",""] ,["S004","Online",450,"","",""] ,["S005","Shop",240,"","",""]];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l5-total-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{width:90},{width:90},{width:90},{width:40},{width:100},{width:130}],minDimensions:[6,6],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l5-total-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l5-total-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l5-total-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l5-total-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l5-total-fb');var rShop=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(5,1,false)||'');var rOn=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(5,2,false)||'');var vShop=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(5,1,true));var vOn=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(5,2,true));var ok=rShop.charAt(0)==='='&&rOn.charAt(0)==='='&&rShop.toUpperCase().indexOf('SUMIF')!==-1&&rOn.toUpperCase().indexOf('SUMIF')!==-1&&Math.abs(vShop-660)<0.1&&Math.abs(vOn-1270)<0.1;if(ok){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct. Shop income is 660 and Online income is 1270. This can become a comparison chart.';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Use SUMIF. Check B2:B6 for the method, then add matching income values from C2:C6.';}};});});});})();

steps.title: Readable Layout
steps.content:
Readable dashboard layout

A dashboard should be easy to read quickly. BTEC tasks reward clear presentation choices that suit the audience.

DoAvoid
Use a clear title for the whole dashboard.Leaving charts untitled.
Use consistent colours and readable font sizes.Using many colours with no meaning.
Place related information near each other.Scattering chart labels randomly.
Show units such as GBP or %.Making viewers guess the units.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l5-layout',[{q:'Which dashboard title is clearest?',options:['Sales Method Income Dashboard','Chart','Data','Sheet 1'],answer:0,right:'Correct. It tells the viewer what the dashboard is about.'},{q:'Which label is clearest for a chart axis?',options:['Income (GBP)','Numbers','Data','Column C'],answer:0,right:'Correct. It includes the meaning and unit.'},{q:'Why keep colours consistent?',options:['So the dashboard is easier to read and compare','So formulas calculate faster','So rows sort automatically','So validation lists disappear'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Consistent design helps the viewer.'},{q:'Which is a poor dashboard choice?',options:['Tiny unlabelled charts with no title','A clear chart title','Readable data labels','A summary table beside its chart'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Tiny unlabelled charts are hard to understand.'}]);

steps.title: Full Dashboard Practice
steps.content:
Full practice: dashboard source data

Complete this source table. It gives a dashboard enough evidence to show counts, income totals and percentages for each sales method.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=17',function(){var data=[["Sale ID","Method","Income","","Method","Count","Income total","% of income"],["S001","Shop",300,"","Shop","","",""] ,["S002","Online",820,"","Online","","",""] ,["S003","Shop",120,"","Total","","=SUM(C2:C6)",""] ,["S004","Online",450,"","",""] ,["S005","Shop",240,"","",""]];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l5-full-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{width:90},{width:90},{width:90},{width:40},{width:90},{width:90},{width:120},{width:120}],minDimensions:[8,6],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l5-full-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l5-full-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l5-full-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l5-full-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l5-full-fb');var checks=[{cell:[5,1],fn:'COUNTIF',exp:3},{cell:[5,2],fn:'COUNTIF',exp:2},{cell:[6,1],fn:'SUMIF',exp:660},{cell:[6,2],fn:'SUMIF',exp:1270},{cell:[7,1],fn:null,exp:34.2},{cell:[7,2],fn:null,exp:65.8}];var errors=[];checks.forEach(function(c){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(c.cell[0],c.cell[1],false)||'');var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(c.cell[0],c.cell[1],true));if(raw.charAt(0)!=='=')errors.push('A source cell needs a formula');else if(c.fn&&raw.toUpperCase().indexOf(c.fn+'(')===-1)errors.push('Use '+c.fn+' in the correct source cell');else if(Math.abs(val-c.exp)>0.6)errors.push('One source value is incorrect');});if(errors.length){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent=errors[0]+' - check COUNTIF, SUMIF and percentage formulas.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct. This source table can support dashboard charts for count, income and percentage split.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Check What You Have Learned
steps.content:
Check what you have learned

These questions check dashboard and chart choices.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l5-exam',[{q:'Which chart is usually best for comparing income across categories?',options:['Bar or column chart','Line chart only','Random picture','No chart'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'},{q:'Which chart is usually best for change over time?',options:['Line chart','Pie chart','Data validation list','Single raw row'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'},{q:'What should a chart source table contain?',options:['Only the labels and calculated values needed for that chart','Every raw row and every blank column','Only colours','Only headings'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'},{q:'Why add data labels or a clear axis?',options:['So viewers can understand the values','So formulas stop calculating','So the chart becomes hidden','So validation lists work'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'},{q:'Which is the clearest chart title?',options:['Income by Sales Method','Chart 1','Data','Thing'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'},{q:'What should dashboard percentages normally include?',options:['A percent symbol and clear label','No label','Only a decimal hidden in the sheet','A random colour only'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'}]);

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 in a row correctly to complete this practice.
steps.js:
(function(){var bank=[{q:'Best chart for comparing categories?',o:['Bar/column chart','Line chart','No chart','Raw table only'],a:0},{q:'Best chart for change over time?',o:['Line chart','Pie chart','Data validation','COUNTIF'],a:0},{q:'What should a dashboard chart have?',o:['Clear title and labels','No title','Hidden values','Random colours'],a:0},{q:'What function can count categories for a chart source table?',o:['COUNTIF','MIN','AVERAGE','IF only'],a:0},{q:'What function can total income by category?',o:['SUMIF','COUNTIF','MAX','Data validation'],a:0},{q:'A source table should be...',o:['Small and focused','As messy as possible','Hidden forever','Only blank cells'],a:0},{q:'A percentage of total is useful because it shows...',o:['Share of the whole','The highest value only','A spelling mistake','A row number'],a:0}];var solved=0,c=document.getElementById('l5-endless');function pick(){return bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)];}function render(){var q=pick();var opts=q.o.map(function(x,i){return{x:x,i:i};}).sort(function(){return Math.random()-0.5;});c.innerHTML='

Correct in a row: '+solved+' / 5

'+q.q+'

'+opts.map(function(o){return '';}).join('')+'
';c.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(function(btn){btn.onclick=function(){var fb=c.querySelector('.ex-feedback');if(parseInt(btn.dataset.i)===q.a){solved++;fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent=solved>=5?'Practice complete.':'Correct. Next question.';if(solved>=5)__markStepComplete();else setTimeout(render,700);}else{solved=0;fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite - streak reset.';setTimeout(render,1000);}};});}render();})();

quizQuestions.q: Which chart is most suitable for comparing income from Shop and Online sales?
Which chart is most suitable for showing satisfaction ratings over four weeks?
In F2 and F3, count how many sales were Shop and Online. These values could be used for a dashboard chart.
In F2 and F3, calculate the total income for Shop and Online. These values could be used for an income comparison chart.
The total income is in G4. In H2 and H3, calculate each method's percentage of total income.
What should a chart source table contain?
options: Only the labels and calculated values needed for that chart
Every raw row and blank column

quizQuestions.q: Why add labels or a value axis to a chart?
options: So the viewer can understand the values being shown
So the chart cannot be edited
